perturbation.original_text: All tangible information, including drawings, specifications and other information furnished hereunder s hall remain the property of Discloser. Upon request, or if either party e lects not to pursue any further business undertaking with the other , Recipient shall promptly return all tangible information, including any and all c opies or partial copies thereof and thereupon confirm destruction of all information held electronically.
perturbation.changed_text: All tangible information, including drawings, specifications and other information furnished hereunder s hall remain the property of Discloser. Upon request, or if either party e lects not to pursue any further business undertaking with the other , Recipient may retain copies of tangible information for archival purposes and is not required to confirm destruction of information held electronically.
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires the Recipient to return all tangible information and confirm the destruction of electronically held information. The modified text allows the Recipient to retain copies and removes the requirement to confirm destruction of electronic data, directly contradicting the obligation to return and destroy all confidential information. This creates uncertainty about the obligations in the original text regarding proprietary information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All tangible information, including drawings, specifications and other information furnished hereunder s hall remain the property of Discloser. Upon request, or if either party e lects not to pursue any further business undertaking with the other , Recipient may retain copies of tangible information for archival purposes and is not required to confirm destruction of information held electronically.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates the Recipient to return all tangible information and confirm the destruction of electronically held information. The modified text allows the Recipient to retain copies and removes the requirement to confirm destruction of electronic data. The contradicted text is a repetition of the Changed text. Since it is a contradiction in itself, and the change allows the recipient to retain information, which goes against returning all tangible information, a strong contradiction exists. This aligns with the Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction definition as a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall become effective as of date hereof and shall terminate upon the express written termination by either one of the parties hereto . Termination of the FLIX ENTERTAINMENT LLC NON -DISCLOSURE CONFIDENTIALITY AGREEMENT - Page 4 of 4 Agreement shall not relieve either party of any obligation set forth in Paragraph s 4 or 6 with respect to Confidential I nformation, and all such obligations shall continue until expiration of the period set forth in Paragrap h 4.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall become effective as of date hereof and shall terminate upon the express written termination by either one of the parties hereto. Termination of the Agreement shall immediately relieve both parties of all obligations, including those in Paragraphs 4 and 6, unless otherwise agreed in writing.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states that termination does not relieve the parties of their obligations regarding confidential information as described in paragraphs 4 and 6. The modified clause states that termination immediately relieves both parties of all obligations, contradicting the original intent of continuing confidentiality even after the agreement ends. This internal contradiction on what happens when the agreement terminates, could lead to ambiguity over proprietary material.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall become effective as of date hereof and shall terminate upon the express written termination by either one of the parties hereto. Termination of the Agreement shall immediately relieve both parties of all obligations, including those in Paragraphs 4 and 6, unless otherwise agreed in writing.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that termination does not relieve the parties of their obligations regarding confidential information as described in paragraphs 4 and 6. The changed clause states that termination immediately relieves both parties of all obligations, contradicting the original intent of continuing confidentiality even after the agreement ends. This internal contradiction on what happens when the agreement terminates, could lead to ambiguity over proprietary material. The contradicted text is the Changed text. The contradiction arises because the original agreement ensures confidentiality even after termination, but the modified text and thus the contradicted text, removes this protection.

perturbation.original_text: Any amendment or modification of this Agreement shall be in writing and executed by a duly authoriz ed representative of either the Discloser or Recipient .
perturbation.changed_text: Any amendment or modification of this Agreement may be made orally and shall be binding upon the parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies that amendments must be in writing. The modified text says they can be oral. This directly contradicts the initial requirement of written amendments. It contradicts the idea of the agreement being amended formally.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Any amendment or modification of this Agreement may be made orally and shall be binding upon the parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates written amendments, while the changed text permits oral amendments. This creates a direct contradiction because the changed text explicitly allows for a method of amendment (oral) that is prohibited by the original text, leading to uncertainty about which method is valid. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, meaning it reinforces the contradiction created by modifying the original text.

perturbation.original_text: Discloser and Recipient agree that it would be impossible or inadequate to measure and calculate Discloser’s damages from any breach of the covenants set forth herein , especially with regard any breach associat ed with Confidential Information that was to be maintained behind the Information Barrier . Accordingly, the Recipient agrees that in the event of a breach of any of the covenants contained in this Agreement, Discloser will have available, in addition to any other right or remedy available, the right to obtain an injunction from a court of competent jurisdiction restraining such breach or threatened breach and to specific performance of any such provision of this Agreement. Recipient further agrees that no bond or other security shall be required in obtaining such equitable relief and that Recipient hereby consents to the issuance of such injunction and to the ordering of specific performance. If any action or proceeding is brought to enforce this Agreement because of an alleged or actual dispute, breach, default, or misrepresentation in connection with any of the provisions of this Agreement, should it prevail, Discloser shall be entitled to recover reasonable attorneys' fees and other costs incurred in suc h action or proceeding in addition to any other relief to which it may be entitled.
perturbation.changed_text: Discloser and Recipient agree that in the event of a breach of any of the covenants contained in this Agreement, Discloser's sole remedy shall be limited to monetary damages not to exceed $1000. Injunctive relief and specific performance are hereby waived by Discloser.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause allows Discloser to seek injunctions and specific performance. The modified clause limits the remedy to monetary damages and waives the right to injunctive relief, directly contradicting the previously established remedies. It creates a direct contradiction regarding how the Discloser can remediate for breach of contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Discloser and Recipient agree that in the event of a breach of any of the covenants contained in this Agreement, Discloser's sole remedy shall be limited to monetary damages not to exceed $1000. Injunctive relief and specific performance are hereby waived by Discloser.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the Discloser's sole remedy is limited to monetary damages and waives injunctive relief and specific performance. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text. This creates a direct conflict, as it explicitly limits the Discloser's remedies, contradicting the original text, which allowed for injunctive relief and specific performance in addition to other remedies. The change causes a structural flaw where the contract now enforces a mutually exclusive remedial action.

perturbation.original_text: For purposes of this Agreement, “Confidential Information” shall mean any propr ietary information belonging to Discloser relating to : (i) Discloser’s proprietary technology , systems, service offering and products, including without limitation, plans and specifications, technical data, trade secrets, know -how, research, product plans, ideas or FLIX ENTERTAINMENT LLC NON -DISCLOSURE CONFIDENTIALITY AGREEMENT - Page 2 of 4 concepts, service s, software, inventions, intellectual property rights , techniques, processes, developments, algorithms, formulas, technology, designs, schematics, drawings, engineering, and hardware configuration information (collectively referred to as “Technical Informa tion”); and (ii) propriet ary information relating to the financial information of any of the principals of Discloser, Discloser’s corporate financial information or its operations and business or financial plans or strategies, including but not limited to its identity, customers, customer lists, markets, real estate development plans, financial statements and projections, products, product pricing and marketing, financial or other strategic business plans or information (collectively referred to as “Busines s Information”) , disclosed to Recipient by Discloser, either directly or indirectly, in writing, orally or by drawings or inspection of samples, equipment or facilities. For purposes of this agreement, Confidential Information shall be assumed to be of a confidential and/or pr oprietary nature by Recipient, as it is reasonable to assume and expect that said Business Information is highly relevant to the success of its Discloser’s business and is not known to the public.
perturbation.changed_text: For purposes of this Agreement, “Confidential Information” shall mean any information disclosed by Discloser to Recipient, regardless of whether it is proprietary or generally known to the public. For purposes of this agreement, Confidential Information shall not be assumed to be of a confidential and/or pr oprietary nature by Recipient, and Recipient will be the one to decide what information will be treated as confidential and/or pr oprietary and communicate this to Discloser. The other conditions in 1a (i) and (ii) still apply
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of Confidential Information is any proprietary information belonging to the Discloser. The modified text defines it as ANY information disclosed, and now the Recipient is the one who decides what is confidential, contradicting the assumption and the nature of what is confidential. It will create uncertainty as to what is confidential and what is not. This change directly impacts the understanding and enforcement of the confidentiality obligations outlined in the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For purposes of this Agreement, “Confidential Information” shall mean any information disclosed by Discloser to Recipient, regardless of whether it is proprietary or generally known to the public. For purposes of this agreement, Confidential Information shall not be assumed to be of a confidential and/or pr oprietary nature by Recipient, and Recipient will be the one to decide what information will be treated as confidential and/or pr oprietary and communicate this to Discloser. The other conditions in 1a (i) and (ii) still apply
perturbation.justification: The original text defines Confidential Information as 'proprietary information belonging to Discloser'. The changed text defines it as 'any information disclosed by Discloser to Recipient, regardless of whether it is proprietary or generally known to the public', and shifts the decision of what is confidential to the Recipient. This directly contradicts the original definition and creates uncertainty as to what is confidential, causing a strong in-text contradiction, as it fundamentally alters the understanding and enforcement of confidentiality obligations. The Contradicted text is the same as the Change Text, reinforcing the contradiction.
